## [1.0.0](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/compare/release-action@v0.1.0...release-action@v1.0.0) (2026-05-29)

## Release Notes

Major release brings automated release approvals and better monorepo support, with standardized bot authentication across all actions.

## ✨ What's New

### Automatic release PR approval
The release workflow can now automatically approve its own pull requests when configured with distinct author and reviewer identities. Previously, auto-approval silently failed when the same bot account tried to approve its own PR. The system now properly detects and reports such configuration errors, ensuring your automated release flow works as intended.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#96: Standardize actions on bot_token and bot_username](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/96)
</details>

### Optional auto-merge for releases
Release pull requests can now be automatically merged after approval by setting `release.automerge: true` in your root `package.json`. When disabled (the default), approved release PRs wait for manual merging, giving teams control over their release timing.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#112: Gate release auto-merge behind a release.automerge flag in package.json](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/112)
</details>

### Ticket tables in monorepo releases
Monorepo release notes now include the same GitHub Issues, Linear, and Jira ticket tables as standalone releases. Each package's release notes show only the tickets relevant to its changes, making it easy to track what issues were addressed in each component.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#102: Monorepo release notes are missing the GitHub Issues / Linear / Jira blocks](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/102)
</details>

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Bot email generation from GitHub API
The action now correctly derives bot user IDs from the GitHub API when generating noreply email addresses, fixing authentication issues that occurred with certain bot configurations.

### Action loading errors
Fixed a critical issue where sync and release composite actions failed to load due to invalid variable expressions in input descriptions.

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes

### Renamed authentication inputs
All `token` and `github_token` inputs are now `bot_token`. Update your workflow files:

**Before:**
```yaml
- uses: awinogradov/code-assistants/.github/actions/release-action@v1
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.GH_TOKEN }}
```

**After:**
```yaml
- uses: awinogradov/code-assistants/.github/actions/release-action@v1
  with:
    bot_token: ${{ secrets.BOT_TOKEN }}
    bot_username: ${{ vars.BOT_USERNAME }}  # optional
```

The new `bot_username` input lets you customize the git author for commits (defaults to `github-actions[bot]`). Internal workflows now use `secrets.BOT_TOKEN` and `vars.BOT_USERNAME` instead of `secrets.GH_TOKEN`.

## 📚 Documentation & Settings Updates

### Release automation constraints
Added comprehensive documentation explaining why release PR authors must use a different identity than code reviewers for auto-approval to work. The release-action README now includes example configurations showing how to pass separate tokens for create and publish modes.

### Auto-merge configuration
New documentation in `docs/release-automerge.md` explains how to enable auto-merge with the `release.automerge` flag and configure the required `release_pr_authors` input for the code-review action.

### ⚠ BREAKING CHANGES

* **actions:** inputs token and github_token are renamed to bot_token; consumers must
update with: blocks to pass bot_token (and optional bot_username), and workflows now read
secrets.BOT_TOKEN and vars.BOT_USERNAME instead of secrets.GH_TOKEN.
